contract.purpose: Guide the operator through emergency or planned relocation of KrotPN to replacement RU/DE servers.
contract.scope: Incident classification, decision points, freeze, backup, replacement deployment checkpoint, restore, validation, cutover, rollback, and evidence capture.

module-map.section-incident-modes: Classifies recovery mode before any action is taken.
module-map.section-decision-points: Defines operator go/no-go choices and required evidence.
module-map.section-replacement-server-runbook: Defines replacement RU/DE deployment checkpoint using existing approved scripts unchanged.
module-map.section-restore-sequence: Defines ordered database, runtime, VPN, migration, and service-start restore sequence.
module-map.section-vpn-validation: Defines tunnel, profile, address-pool, client compatibility, and rotation-guard checks.
module-map.section-live-boundaries: Separates local dry-run evidence from live VPS evidence.

change-summary.change-1: v1.0.0 - Phase-22 step-1 created incident modes and operator decision points.
change-summary.change-2: v1.1.0 - Phase-22 step-2 added maintenance/freeze sequence and backup evidence handoff.
change-summary.change-3: v1.2.0 - Phase-22 step-5 added replacement-server runbook and restore checkpoint.
change-summary.change-4: v1.3.0 - Phase-22 step-6 added ordered restore sequence and service-start gate.
change-summary.change-5: v1.4.0 - Phase-22 step-7 added VPN-specific validation and rotation guard.

mode-1.trigger: RU and/or DE server is unavailable and old-server rollback may be impossible.
mode-1.operator-decision: Start emergency restore from the latest verified backup bundle; record backup age and expected data loss window.
mode-1.rollback-eligibility: Rollback is unavailable unless the old provider or snapshots recover before cutover.

mode-2.trigger: Operator intentionally moves service to replacement RU/DE servers.
mode-2.operator-decision: Schedule maintenance, freeze risky writes, capture fresh backup, deploy replacement servers, then cut over only after validation.
mode-2.rollback-eligibility: Rollback remains available while old servers and DNS/IP target are still controlled by the operator.

mode-3.trigger: RU entry node or RU-hosted application stack fails while DE exit may still be healthy.
mode-3.operator-decision: Prioritize RU replacement because user/API/admin surfaces and entry tunnel terminate there.
mode-3.rollback-eligibility: Rollback depends on RU host recoverability and preservation of database/runtime state.

mode-4.trigger: DE exit node fails while RU application stack is still reachable.
mode-4.operator-decision: Replace DE, validate RU-DE tunnel and exit egress, and avoid client rotation unless compatibility checks fail.
mode-4.rollback-eligibility: Rollback is possible if old DE recovers and tunnel profile parity is preserved.

mode-5.trigger: Operator suspects server compromise, leaked secret material, or untrusted host access.
mode-5.operator-decision: Relocate with explicit secret rotation decision; preserve data only after assessing backup trust.
mode-5.rollback-eligibility: Rollback to old servers is blocked unless compromise is disproven.

decision-1.name: enter-maintenance
decision-1.required-evidence: Incident mode, operator identity, latest backup age, old-server availability, and user-impact note.
decision-1.go-condition: Operator confirms recovery or migration is safer than waiting.
decision-1.abort-condition: Incident is transient and old servers can be safely restored without relocation.

decision-2.name: backup-or-use-existing-bundle
decision-2.required-evidence: Fresh backup availability or explicit reason why only an existing bundle can be used.
decision-2.go-condition: Backup bundle exists and its manifest can be checked before restore.
decision-2.abort-condition: No trusted backup exists and operator has not accepted data-loss risk.

decision-3.name: deploy-replacement
decision-3.required-evidence: Target RU/DE IPs, OS readiness, SSH access, and confirmation that existing deploy/install scripts will be used unchanged.
decision-3.go-condition: Replacement hosts are ready and no script redesign is required.
decision-3.abort-condition: Deployment would require unplanned changes to deploy/ or install.sh.

decision-4.name: restore-validation
decision-4.required-evidence: Manifest checksums, table counts, migration state, AWG profile parity, and no-secret-log review.
decision-4.go-condition: All restore checks pass or operator records an explicit risk waiver.
decision-4.abort-condition: Critical counts, checksums, or client-compatibility checks fail.

decision-5.name: cutover
decision-5.required-evidence: Backend, frontend, admin, auth, billing read path, Redis, RU-DE tunnel, and existing client/device validation.
decision-5.go-condition: Replacement deployment is healthier than the old target and rollback status is known.
decision-5.abort-condition: Any critical health, auth, payment-read, or VPN handshake gate is missing.

maintenance-freeze-handoff.handoff-1: Before backup capture, follow docs/recovery/BACKUP-PHASE-22.xml maintenance-freeze steps in order.
maintenance-freeze-handoff.handoff-2: Do not start replacement deployment until pre-backup evidence names app version, old RU/DE addresses, table counts, migration state, and rollback candidate status.
maintenance-freeze-handoff.handoff-3: If old servers are unavailable, record why fresh backup capture is impossible and use the latest trusted backup bundle only after operator accepts data-loss risk.

stage-1.name: target-preflight
stage-1.operator-action: Confirm replacement RU and DE hosts, OS baseline, SSH access, DNS/IP plan, and whether old servers remain rollback candidates.
stage-1.required-evidence: target_hosts.md with RU/DE addresses, provider names, access owner, and rollback status.
stage-1.blocked-if: Target hosts require unplanned changes to deploy/, install.sh, nginx, or docker-compose.yml before restore.

stage-2.name: fresh-deploy
stage-2.operator-action: Run the existing operator-approved install/deploy flow exactly as currently documented for a fresh RU/DE deployment.
stage-2.required-evidence: deploy_notes.md states which existing script entrypoint was used and records only success/failure, timestamps, hostnames, and non-secret fingerprints.
stage-2.blocked-if: Any recovery step requires editing deploy scripts or changing the approved password-based deployment flow.

stage-3.name: restore-checkpoint-stop
stage-3.operator-action: After replacement deployment boots, stop at restore checkpoint before public cutover or user traffic is accepted.
stage-3.required-evidence: restore_checkpoint.md confirms backend/database services are ready for restore, public cutover has not happened, and secrets were not printed.
stage-3.blocked-if: Replacement deployment is already serving production traffic before database/runtime/VPN restore validation.

stage-4.name: handoff-to-restore
stage-4.operator-action: Hand off to restore sequence only after the backup manifest, checksums, and target-host evidence are available.
stage-4.blocked-if: Backup bundle is missing or restore target cannot be isolated from users during restore.

restore-1.name: verify-bundle-before-restore
restore-1.operator-action: Verify manifest and checksums before loading any data into the replacement environment.
restore-1.required-evidence: checksums.sha256 pass, manifest.json present, and restore_notes.md started.
restore-1.blocked-if: Any required bundle artifact is missing or checksum verification fails.

restore-2.name: restore-runtime-env
restore-2.operator-action: Restore protected runtime environment material to the replacement deployment without printing secret values.
restore-2.required-evidence: runtime env keys present as booleans/fingerprints only; no raw secret output.
restore-2.blocked-if: Backend, database, Redis, billing, Telegram, JWT, or encryption keys are missing.

restore-3.name: restore-postgres
restore-3.operator-action: Restore postgres.dump into the replacement PostgreSQL target while public traffic remains blocked.
restore-3.required-evidence: restore_notes.md records dump file name, restore status, table_counts.after.json, and no secret output.
restore-3.blocked-if: Database restore exits with errors or critical table counts cannot be captured.

restore-4.name: restore-vpn-state
restore-4.operator-action: Restore AWG profiles, subnet settings, server key material, peer state, and device-client linkage from vpn-state.tar.enc.
restore-4.required-evidence: vpn_fingerprints.after.json matches expected profile fingerprints and peer counts within explicit tolerance.
restore-4.blocked-if: Restored VPN state would silently invalidate existing client configs.

restore-5.name: verify-migration-state
restore-5.operator-action: Compare source and restored migration state, then run compatibility checks before application startup.
restore-5.required-evidence: migration_state.after.txt is present and matches or supersedes the source state.
restore-5.blocked-if: Migration state is older than source or incompatible with restored database contents.

restore-6.name: start-services-after-validation
restore-6.operator-action: Start services only after restore integrity gates pass; keep cutover blocked until application and VPN validation complete.
restore-6.required-evidence: service_start_notes.md with startup status and link to validation checklist.
restore-6.blocked-if: Restore integrity gates are incomplete, waived without operator note, or failed.

vpn-check-1.name: ru-de-tunnel-health
vpn-check-1.required-evidence: RU and DE AWG interfaces are up, relay addresses are reachable, and tunnel handshake is fresh enough for operator-defined tolerance.
vpn-check-1.failure-action: Do not cut over; inspect host-level AWG/routing state on replacement RU/DE.

vpn-check-2.name: awg-profile-parity
vpn-check-2.required-evidence: Restored CLIENT_PROFILE and RELAY_PROFILE fingerprints match the expected bundle fingerprints; raw keys are not printed.
vpn-check-2.failure-action: Stop validation because client and relay handshakes may fail after cutover.

vpn-check-3.name: address-pool-compatibility
vpn-check-3.required-evidence: Restored VPN_CLIENT_SUBNET, VPN_RELAY_SUBNET, VPN_CAPACITY_PROFILE, and stored VPNClient addresses match M-032 capacity and no-silent-migration rules.
vpn-check-3.failure-action: Abort cutover unless operator explicitly approves address-pool rotation and client reprovisioning.

vpn-check-4.name: existing-client-config-behavior
vpn-check-4.required-evidence: At least one existing user/device config path is validated without forced rotation; generated config still uses the restored address and profile values.
vpn-check-4.failure-action: Do not cut over because existing users may lose connectivity.

vpn-check-5.name: explicit-rotation-guard
vpn-check-5.required-evidence: restore_notes.md records VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE state. If rotation is needed, operator approval and client communication plan are attached.
vpn-check-5.failure-action: Block any silent client address/profile rewrite.

live-boundaries.local-only: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, manifest schema review, and non-production dry-run can prove document and procedure consistency.
live-boundaries.live-required: Replacement VPS deployment, RU-DE tunnel health, DNS/IP cutover, and existing user config connectivity require live or restore-target evidence.
